symbols.name: a
symbols.def: Treatment level indexing a potential outcome or outcome regression.

symbols.name: P_X
symbols.def: Marginal law of X induced by P.

symbols.name: Y(a)
symbols.def: Potential outcome under treatment level a.

symbols.name: r_0
symbols.def: Radius of the design-regularity neighborhood of x_0.

symbols.name: alpha
symbols.def: Holder order of the propensity score.

symbols.name: beta
symbols.def: Holder order of each outcome regression.

symbols.name: gamma
symbols.def: Holder order of the CATE function.

symbols.name: H^s([0,1]^d,L)
symbols.def: Holder ball of order s and radius L.

symbols.name: epsilon_n
symbols.def: Deterministic central privacy budget at sample size n.

symbols.name: delta_n
symbols.def: Deterministic approximate-privacy failure probability at sample size n.

symbols.name: M_n
symbols.def: Possibly randomized release based on the observed sample.

symbols.name: D,D'
symbols.space: pairs of samples in ([-1,1] x {0,1} x [0,1]^d)^n
symbols.def: Datasets differing in exactly one record.

symbols.name: R_n^DP
symbols.def: Central-DP minimax expected absolute error for tau_P(x_0).

symbols.name: r_n^CATE
symbols.def: Kennedy--Balakrishnan--Robins--Wasserman's non-private pointwise benchmark n^(-kappa) in the regime 0<beta<=gamma.

symbols.name: r_n^regDP
symbols.def: Central-DP pointwise-regression calibration inf_{0<h<=r_0}{h^gamma+(n h^d)^(-1/2)+1/(n epsilon_n h^d)}.

symbols.name: P_{alpha,beta,gamma}(L,e_0,f_0,f_1,r_0,x_0)
symbols.def: Observational Holder CATE class defined in def:holder-cate-class.

assumptions.condition: (Y(0),Y(1)) independent of A conditional on X.
e_0 <= pi_P(x) <= 1-e_0 for all x in [0,1]^d.
f_0 <= p_P(x) <= f_1 for all x in {u in [0,1]^d: ||u-x_0||_infinity<=r_0}.
Pr{M_n(D) in B} <= exp(epsilon_n) Pr{M_n(D') in B}+delta_n for all D,D',B.
L>e_0, so the propensity Holder ball contains an overlap-preserving interior constant and sufficiently small compactly supported alpha-smooth perturbations around it.

novel.justification: standard: interior-radius condition for Holder minimax lower bounds

definitions.name: P_{alpha,beta,gamma}(L,e_0,f_0,f_1,r_0,x_0)
definitions.construction: {P : P satisfies ass:consistency, ass:exchangeability, ass:overlap, ass:pi-holder, ass:mu-holder, ass:tau-holder, ass:smoothness-order, and ass:local-density}

definitions.name: R_n^DP(P_{alpha,beta,gamma},epsilon_n,delta_n,x_0)
definitions.construction: inf_{M_n: M_n satisfies ass:central-dp} sup_{P in P_{alpha,beta,gamma}(L,e_0,f_0,f_1,r_0,x_0)} E_P[|M_n(O_1,...,O_n)-tau_P(x_0)|]

definitions.name: causal private frontier handle
definitions.construction: Let q=alpha+beta and define the formal algebraic objective E_n^alg(h,k)=h^gamma+(h/k^{1/d})^q+(n h^d)^(-1/2)+sqrt(k)/(n h^d)+k/(n epsilon_n h^d), over 0<h<=r_0 and 1<=k<=n h^d. Its exactly optimized value is rho_n^alg=r_n^CATE vee (n epsilon_n)^(-gamma/(gamma+d)) vee (n epsilon_n)^(-q/(q+d)); with s=min(gamma,q), this is n^(-kappa) vee (n epsilon_n)^(-s/(s+d)), and epsilon_n^alg=n^(-1+kappa(s+d)/s) is only its algebraic crossing. Neither E_n^alg nor rho_n^alg is a proved risk envelope, attainable rate, minimax frontier, or minimax elbow. The only certified full-class statement is lem:certified-private-cate-bracket, with sharp corollaries lem:equal-smoothness-sharp-private-rate and lem:bounded-effective-private-sample-risk. A private-HOIF upper program must separately privatize or globally stabilize nuisance-pilot, Gram, first-order-score, and second-order folds and retain the KBRW Gram/density third-order remainder. For q<gamma, a positive-density fuzzy family plus an approximate-DP hypercube inequality is one possible converse program, but lem:general-two-point-barrier proves only that a two-point argument certifying indistinguishability through n{exp(epsilon_n)-1+delta_n}TV(P,Q)<=eta<1 cannot exceed the gamma privacy branch; it does not show that every route to the q branch must use fuzzy cancellation. When n epsilon_n=1 the formal q and gamma privacy powers coincide; the q power is strictly larger only when n epsilon_n>1. The KBRW nonprivate expression is not asserted as the established frontier of the full positive-density class: its published lower construction and upper attainability conditions concern different density scopes, with matching on a density/Gram-stable submodel.

statements.statement: For deterministic sequences epsilon_n in [n^(-1),1] and 0<delta_n<=n^(-2), determine the sharp central-(epsilon_n,delta_n)-DP pointwise CATE risk R_n^DP(P_{alpha,beta,gamma},epsilon_n,delta_n,x_0) over P_{alpha,beta,gamma}(L,e_0,f_0,f_1,r_0,x_0). Establish a two-sided rate and phase diagram; determine whether the privacy-free limit over this full positive-density class reduces to r_n^CATE (open here; the Kennedy--Balakrishnan--Robins--Wasserman match uses a density/Gram-stable submodel), and decide whether the pointwise-regression calibration r_n^regDP and private estimation of pi_P, mu_{0,P}, and mu_{1,P} generate one or more additional leading regimes. Prove any lower bound using a causal localized family rather than a regression stand-in.
statements.justification: Central privacy changes both local-score sensitivity and nuisance learning, neither of which is resolved by the non-private causal rate or a private regression calibration alone.
statements.gap: KennedyBalakrishnanRobinsWasserman2024MinimaxCATE, Theorem 1 and Remark 2 (p. 4), gives the non-private pointwise rate in the regime gamma>=beta and separates its bounded-density lower model from its stable-density attainability submodel. CaiChakrabortyVuursteen2024OptimalFederated, Theorems 3.4 and 4.4 (pp. 14, 17), gives pointwise private regression bounds, including a one-server central-DP specialization, but no causal nuisance geometry. SchroderMelnychukFeuerriegel2025DPCATE and NiuNoriQuistorffEtAl2022DPHeterogeneous give private CATE procedures without this pointwise causal minimax frontier.
statements.consumer: The frontier supplies a rate-optimal privacy-budget and localization rule for releasing an observational treatment effect at a fixed covariate profile and a minimax benchmark for private CATE learners.

sampling_model.design: iid observational sampling under central curator privacy
sampling_model.nuisance_estimation: The model imposes only law-level Holder regularity. The handle studies cross-fitted private estimation of pi_P, mu_{0,P}, and mu_{1,P}, with privacy composition across nuisance and local-score releases.

tldr: The project asks for the sharp central approximate-differential-privacy minimax risk of a Holder-smooth pointwise observational CATE in the Kennedy--Balakrishnan--Robins--Wasserman regime 0<beta<=gamma. The established non-private causal benchmark and the known central-private pointwise-regression calibration are treated as barriers, not as new results. The unresolved question is whether privately learning causal nuisances creates a further leading regime; its resolution requires a private higher-order local R-score upper bound and a lower bound that preserves joint CATE, propensity, and outcome-regression perturbations.

project_justification.gap: No published result characterizes the central-(epsilon_n,delta_n)-DP pointwise minimax risk of an observational Holder CATE while retaining the causal nuisance geometry of the non-private problem.
project_justification.niche: The missing object is a smoothness--dimension--privacy phase frontier for a local causal functional, where privacy affects both localization and nuisance adjustment.
project_justification.fill: The project poses the exact observational frontier, with a concrete private higher-order local-score and causal-DP-tracing handle for its matching upper and lower bounds.

related_work: Kennedy, Balakrishnan, Robins, and Wasserman (2024), Theorem 1 and Remark 2 (p. 4), derive the non-private Holder pointwise CATE rate in the regime gamma>=beta and distinguish the bounded-density lower model from the density-qualified upper-bound model; this is the exact privacy-free reduction required here. Cai, Chakraborty, and Vuursteen (2024), Theorems 3.4 and 4.4 (pp. 14, 17), provide pointwise private nonparametric-regression rates in a federated architecture whose one-server specialization calibrates the central-DP regression barrier, but it does not contain causal nuisance functions or a causal lower-bound family. Niu et al. (2022), Section 3 and Theorem 6 (pp. 3--6), provide privacy accounting for sample-split CATE meta-learners without nonparametric pointwise minimax theory. Schroder, Melnychuk, and Feuerriegel (2025), Theorems 1--3 (pp. 5--7), provide Neyman-orthogonal DP CATE releases, not a Holder pointwise causal upper/lower frontier. Dwork and Roth (2014) provide the central approximate-DP experiment used in the risk definition.
honest_scope: The central-DP pointwise-regression calibration is published comparison material, not a claimed causal theorem. The open content is the full observational frontier: whether private propensity and outcome-regression learning changes the leading rate beyond the non-private causal and regression-privacy barriers, and a causal-DP converse that resolves that question.

bibliography.citation: Kennedy, E. H., Balakrishnan, S., Robins, J. M., and Wasserman, L. (2024). Minimax rates for heterogeneous causal effect estimation. Annals of Statistics 52(2), 793--816. arXiv:2203.00837.
Cai, T. T., Chakraborty, A., and Vuursteen, L. (2024). Optimal federated learning for nonparametric regression with heterogeneous distributed differential privacy constraints. arXiv:2406.06755.
Schroder, M., Melnychuk, V., and Feuerriegel, S. (2025). Differentially private learners for heterogeneous treatment effects. ICLR. arXiv:2503.03486.
Niu, F., Nori, H., Quistorff, B., Caruana, R., Ngwe, D., and Kannan, A. (2022). Differentially private estimation of heterogeneous causal effects. CLeaR, PMLR 177, 618--633. arXiv:2202.11043.
Dwork, C., and Roth, A. (2014). The Algorithmic Foundations of Differential Privacy. Foundations and Trends in Theoretical Computer Science 9(3--4), 211--407.
Imbens, G. W., and Rubin, D. B. (2015). Causal Inference for Statistics, Social, and Biomedical Sciences. Cambridge University Press.

comparator_promise_table.comparator_claim: Derives the non-private Holder pointwise CATE minimax rate in the gamma>=beta regime and attains it on a density-qualified submodel.
Obtains pointwise private nonparametric-regression bounds up to logarithms under heterogeneous federated privacy constraints, with a one-server central-DP specialization.
Provides a Neyman-orthogonal DP CATE framework and private finite- and functional-query releases without a pointwise causal minimax theorem.
Provides DP CATE meta-algorithms with sample splitting and parallel composition.

novelty_justification: The proposal no longer claims a randomized-treatment privacy theorem, because the central-DP regression calibration is covered by Cai, Chakraborty, and Vuursteen's one-server specialization. Its sole research claim is the unresolved observational CATE frontier. Relative to Kennedy et al., it adds central privacy while retaining their gamma>=beta causal point-functional regime; relative to Cai et al., it requires causal nuisance adjustment and a causal indistinguishability construction; relative to existing DP CATE work, it asks for matching pointwise minimax upper and lower bounds.

literature_checklist.author: Kennedy, Balakrishnan, Robins, and Wasserman
literature_checklist.one_line: Theorem 1 and Remark 2 (p. 4), Section 4, and Appendix B.8 establish the non-private pointwise benchmark and density-qualified attainability scope in the gamma>=beta regime.

literature_checklist.author: Cai, Chakraborty, and Vuursteen
literature_checklist.one_line: Theorems 3.4 and 4.4 (pp. 14, 17) give private pointwise regression rates and supply the one-server central-DP calibration, not a causal CATE result.

literature_checklist.author: Schroder, Melnychuk, and Feuerriegel
literature_checklist.one_line: Theorems 1--3 (pp. 5--7) provide private CATE mechanisms without a Holder pointwise minimax frontier.

literature_checklist.author: Niu, Nori, Quistorff, Caruana, Ngwe, and Kannan
literature_checklist.one_line: Section 3 and Theorem 6 (pp. 3--6) establish privacy composition for CATE meta-learners rather than pointwise minimax theory.

literature_checklist.author: Dwork and Roth
literature_checklist.venue: Foundations and Trends in Theoretical Computer Science
literature_checklist.one_line: Defines the central approximate-DP neighboring-database experiment used in the minimax risk.
